decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates invasive pneumococcal disease incidence from two reporting sources using capture-recapture analysis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to pneumococcal disease surveillance and vaccine-impact estimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs a three-source capture–recapture analysis to estimate ascertainment of food-borne outbreak events.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to norovirus, hepatitis A, and Shigella outbreak ascertainment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses capture-recapture analysis across three registries to estimate completeness and meningococcal disease burden.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to meningococcal disease surveillance and vaccination assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a two-way capture-recapture analysis to estimate registry completeness and TB-HIV prevalence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to TB-HIV co-infection surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs retrospective and prospective capture-recapture matching to identify missed perinatal hepatitis B pairs.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to perinatal hepatitis B surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses capture-recapture across four data systems to estimate invasive meningococcal disease and source completeness.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to meningococcal disease surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses capture-recapture analyses to estimate completeness of pertussis hospitalization and death reporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to pertussis surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Matches hospital and statutory surveillance cases and estimates pertussis incidence using capture-recapture methodology.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to hospitalized pertussis surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Adjusts narcolepsy underreporting using cases from two sources and capture-recapture methods.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The application concerns a vaccine-associated narcolepsy safety outcome rather than infectious-disease burden or surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies capture-recapture models to overlapping healthcare and surveillance lists to estimate HIV registry completeness.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to HIV surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses two-source active surveillance with capture-recapture correction when estimating pneumococcal disease incidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to invasive pneumococcal disease surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Conducts two capture/recapture studies to estimate the population size of people who inject drugs.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete population-size application supporting HIV prevention planning.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses capture-recapture methods to assess dengue underreporting within facility-based fever surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to dengue surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a three-dataset capture-recapture technique to estimate norovirus seafood-outbreak burden.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to norovirus outbreak surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses capture-recapture methods on notification and hospitalization data to estimate tick-borne encephalitis ascertainment.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to tick-borne encephalitis surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Carries out two-way capture-recapture analysis on national registries to estimate acute hepatitis C infections.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to hepatitis C surveillance among adults with HIV.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs capture-recapture analysis to evaluate identification of births to hepatitis B-positive women.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to perinatal hepatitis B surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Chapman's capture-recapture method and log-linear modeling to estimate neonatal herpes incidence and mortality.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to neonatal herpes surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a modified Chao capture-recapture estimator, derives its variance, and applies it to undetected COVID-19 infections.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development source for capture-recapture estimation under heterogeneous detection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses capture-recapture with log-linear modeling across three sources to estimate malaria-related deaths.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to imported-malaria mortality surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs three-source log-linear capture-recapture analysis to estimate pneumococcal disease underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to invasive pneumococcal disease surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the Petersen capture-recapture method on two databases to estimate influenza hospitalization incidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to influenza hospitalization surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies Chapman and Chao capture-recapture estimators to two sources of foot-and-mouth disease outbreaks.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to animal infectious-disease surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses capture-recapture on two hospital registries to estimate missed orthopedic surgical-site infections.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to surgical-site infection surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a capture-recapture approach, with misclassification adjustment, to estimate arbovirus reporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to dengue, chikungunya, and Zika surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a two-source capture-recapture method to estimate regional chronic hepatitis C prevalence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to hepatitis C prevalence estimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the capture-recapture method and Chapman estimator to assess Guillain-Barré syndrome surveillance sensitivity.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The target is neurological-syndrome surveillance rather than a direct infectious-disease application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies unilist capture-recapture methods to estimate Ebola contact-tracing completeness.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to Ebola outbreak contact tracing.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a three-source capture-recapture method and log-linear model to estimate tuberculosis reporting completeness.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to pulmonary tuberculosis surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies uni-list capture-recapture models to estimate COVID-19 contact-tracing sensitivity.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to COVID-19 contact tracing.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Designs and implements shinyrecap, a web interface supporting capture-recapture population-size estimation and diagnostics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit software implementation for capture-recapture analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a two-system capture-recapture model to estimate meningococcal disease incidence and surveillance sensitivity.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to meningococcal disease surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a novel capture-recapture estimator combining representative sampling with nonrepresentative surveillance streams.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development source for hybrid capture-recapture case-count estimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a ratio-regression capture-recapture approach and demonstrates it with COVID-19 contact-tracing data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological proposal for capture-recapture completeness estimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses log-linear capture-recapture analysis after probabilistic database linkage to estimate undiagnosed HIV cases.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to HIV surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Combines independent surveillance systems with capture-recapture methods to estimate influenza hospitalization rates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to multi-season influenza surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops partially identified, dependence-robust confidence sets for capture-recapture population estimates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development addressing unverifiable sample-dependence assumptions.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Benchmarks a targeted minimum loss-based capture-recapture estimator against common models and ground truth.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit performance evaluation of a capture-recapture estimator.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses capture-recapture methods on two surveillance systems to estimate adult RSV hospitalizations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to respiratory syncytial virus surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a capture-recapture framework incorporating expert opinion about dependencies between surveillance streams.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development for dependency-sensitive disease-surveillance estimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses capture-recapture alongside back-casting to estimate underdetected COVID-19 infections in Kazakhstan.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to COVID-19 burden estimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian model averaging for capture-recapture estimation across three monitoring systems.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to carbapenemase-producing Enterobacterales infection surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a new capture-recapture strategy that adjusts for diagnostic-test misclassification and develops interval inference.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development for misclassification-adjusted infectious-disease surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses two-source capture-recapture analysis to estimate acute flaccid paralysis case ascertainment.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to surveillance intended to detect possible poliovirus infection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: This is a study protocol describing a planned future application of capture-recapture models, without completed use or results.
decisions.lineage_evidence: A planned protocol is not yet a demonstrated methodological or infectious-disease application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies two capture-recapture methods to linked medical-record sources to estimate chlamydia and gonorrhea burden.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to sexually transmitted infection surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Investigates a case-reported absent assay control line and experimentally identifies high-molecular-weight interference as the likely failure mechanism.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit diagnostic investigation of a dengue-assay failure reported in a patient case.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes power and sample-size methods for case-series models with exposure-onset measurement error.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development and design guidance for the exact model class.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes measurement error case-series models and bias-corrected estimation for imprecise exposure-onset timing.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development source for exposure-timing-error case-series models.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates the validity and power of naive tests when case-series exposure-onset times contain measurement error.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit simulation and theoretical performance evaluation for the exact model class.
supported_label_variants: case series analysis with time-varying exposure onset measurement error

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a self-controlled case-series modification for pharmacovigilance underreporting, not exposure-onset timing error.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The modification addresses a neighboring reporting problem rather than the exact exposure-timing-error concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a modified self-controlled case-series model for passive-reporting processes, without exposure-timing-error modeling.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The paper concerns reporting-process adjustment rather than the exact exposure-timing-error model class.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares cases detected by passive and enhanced surveillance and identifies underreporting and detection differences.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit evaluation of enhanced surveillance performance for pertussis detection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares dengue incidence detected in an actively followed cohort with passive national surveillance estimates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit comparative evaluation of active versus passive dengue surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses frequent household visits and respiratory sampling for active community-based influenza surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete community application to influenza incidence and seasonality.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Follows a community cohort with weekly fever contacts, home sampling, and annual serology to ascertain dengue incidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete active community ascertainment of dengue infection.
named_source_mentions: A cohort study to assess the incidence of dengue, Brazil, 2014-2018

decisions.relationship_evidence: Conducts active longitudinal surveillance of deployed personnel to ascertain travelers' diarrhea and pathogens.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete active-cohort application to infectious diarrhea.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reports a clinical and entomological flavivirus investigation but does not describe community active infection surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Community sampling alone does not establish use of the exact surveillance concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses weekly contacts, home visits, and laboratory testing in a prospective community cohort to detect dengue.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete community-based active dengue surveillance application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs weekly sentinel testing for SARS-CoV-2 in schools, kindergartens, and nurseries.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete active testing application in educational communities.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Tests sera collected from febrile patients but does not describe an active community surveillance design.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The cross-sectional clinical investigation is not an explicit use of the exact surveillance concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Actively screens children and staff across 17 schools during a 10-week sentinel-surveillance cohort study.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete active SARS-CoV-2 surveillance application in school communities.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses symptom diaries in a birth cohort but does not identify the work as community active infection surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Symptom-diary burden assessment is insufficient to establish the exact surveillance concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses active household case finding for wounds broadly, rather than active surveillance of an infectious-disease outcome.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The broader wound survey is not a direct application of the exact infection-surveillance concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Repeatedly follows a population-based household cohort with questionnaires and SARS-CoV-2 antibody testing.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete longitudinal community application to SARS-CoV-2 infection monitoring.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Implements weekly active surveillance, scheduled visits, and illness-triggered testing in a community dengue cohort.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete active community dengue surveillance application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses repeat serologic testing in a community prospective cohort to estimate SARS-CoV-2 infection incidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete longitudinal community ascertainment of SARS-CoV-2 infection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses twice-weekly PCR pool testing as sentinel surveillance across primary and special-needs schools.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete active SARS-CoV-2 surveillance application in school communities.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a one-time online survey of recommended home tests rather than an active community surveillance system.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The survey estimates underreporting but is not the exact active-surveillance design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Conducts a cross-sectional prevalence survey shortly after community transmission, not ongoing active surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: A single prevalence assessment is not a demonstrated use of the exact surveillance concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Implements longitudinal weekly testing-based sentinel surveillance for three respiratory viruses.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete active infection-surveillance application in workplace cohorts.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Conducts five rounds of population serology and viral sequencing to monitor SARS-CoV-2 infection dynamics over two years.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete longitudinal population monitoring of SARS-CoV-2 infection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Conducts a single representative cross-sectional prevalence survey, not an ongoing community active surveillance design.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Measurement of active infection at one time point is not active infection surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Conducts statewide community serosurveillance with randomized cluster sampling and linked mosquito-pool surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete community dengue surveillance application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Visits households biweekly for symptom reporting, nasal sampling, and periodic serology in population-based surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete active community surveillance of SARS-CoV-2 infection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Collects weekly throat swabs regardless of symptoms in a longitudinal school and childcare cohort.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete active ascertainment of SARS-CoV-2 and influenza infections in educational communities.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes a cross-sectional convenience collection of residual sera rather than conducting active community surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The seroprevalence analysis is not the exact surveillance design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Conducts serially timed cross-sectional serosurveys from referral-center samples, not community active surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The healthcare-sample serosurvey does not establish the exact community surveillance concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a statewide cluster-randomized community survey with human serology and longitudinal vector-surveillance comparisons.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete community dengue monitoring application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a small online school survey and dashboard comparison, not community active infection surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The survey documents underreporting without implementing the exact surveillance design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs a population-based serosurvey during one study round rather than active longitudinal infection surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Population representativeness alone does not establish the exact active-surveillance concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Conducts a cross-sectional community seroprevalence study without an active surveillance process.
decisions.lineage_evidence: One-time community sampling is not the exact surveillance concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Implements active sentinel surveillance at health facilities and points of entry, not community-based surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The setting is a neighboring facility/entry sentinel design rather than the exact community concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Implements community-based sentinel surveillance through community organizations with screening and laboratory testing.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete community surveillance application for mpox-like disease in high-risk populations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and applies county-specific correction factors to reconcile inflated circumcision coverage and denominator problems.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicitly reports development of transferable modeling methods for coverage correction.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a micro-census to test target-population denominator estimates underlying administrative immunization coverage.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit validation of coverage denominators against direct enumeration.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Triangulates program, fertility, survey, and facility-birth data to adjust inflated antenatal denominators and HIV estimates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to HIV and prevention-of-mother-to-child-transmission coverage estimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Implements a dormant-record state in an immunization registry to reduce denominator inflation in coverage estimates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit registry implementation for denominator correction.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses numbers tested and proportions positive to distinguish infection trends from changes in stool-sampling rates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to Campylobacter and Salmonella laboratory surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Calculates test positivity using total tests as the denominator to interpret changing STEC notifications.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete denominator-adjusted analysis of STEC surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Contextualizes Legionnaires' disease notifications with test-volume denominators and analyzes positivity over time.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete denominator-based analysis of Legionnaires' disease surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Validates reported patient-day and central-line-day denominators against blinded retrospective chart review.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit accuracy evaluation of surveillance denominator data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Prospectively compares weekly sampled denominator counts and derived infection rates with daily collection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit multicenter performance evaluation of denominator sampling.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates denominator-data completeness, representativeness, and accuracy against administrative data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit validation of surgical-site infection surveillance denominators.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Summarizes and compares HIV surveillance reports, trends, demographic patterns, and transmission distributions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete descriptive analysis of HIV/AIDS surveillance patterns.
named_source_mentions: end-year reports of HIV/AIDS Surveillance in Europe

decisions.relationship_evidence: Describes outbreak epidemiological features including attack risk, underreporting, and movement risk.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete descriptive analysis of equine influenza outbreak patterns.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares age- and sex-stratified pneumococcal disease incidence and serotype patterns across vaccine periods.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete descriptive surveillance analysis of invasive pneumococcal disease.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses ten point-prevalence surveys to describe infection prevalence changes and associated patient factors.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete descriptive surveillance analysis of healthcare-associated infections.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reports device-associated infection rates, utilization, resistance, mortality, and comparisons across surveillance systems.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete descriptive analysis of healthcare-associated infection surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes global incidence, age, vaccination, completeness, and regional patterns in diphtheria surveillance data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete descriptive surveillance analysis of global diphtheria epidemiology.
